summary: Option-income ETFs have become one of the fastest-growing segments of the ETF market, but headline yields often obscure meaningful differences in portfolio construction, risk profile and objectives. In this episode of Inside Active, host David Cohne, mutual fund and active management analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, speaks with John Burrello, senior portfolio manager for Invesco’s Global Strategies team, including the Invesco QQQ Income Advantage (QQA), about how investors should evaluate thes

title: Mizuho Partners With Bank of Baroda to Finance India Deals
summary: India’s Bank of Baroda has partnered with Mizuho Bank Ltd., one of Japan’s largest lenders, to jointly fund mergers and acquisitions, signaling expectations that deal activity will accelerate in the world’s fastest-growing large economy.

title: Europe Startups in Search of Cash Opt for SPAC Route
summary: Nine European startups have announced plans to merge with US blank check firms this year.

title: Stocks Hover, Best Quarter Since 2020, Indirect US-Iran Talks
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 0:00

title: Britain’s Big Plans for Small Ships
summary: The UK’s future large, advanced destroyers ran aground on the shores of fiscal austerity. Instead, the Royal Navy will get a bunch of domestically made smaller vessels and network them with drones.

title: Deutsche Bank Sells India Units to Kotak for $30 Million
summary: Deutsche Bank AG agreed to sell its Indian consumer banking and wealth businesses to Kotak Mahindra Bank for around 2.82 billion rupees ($30 million), joining a slew of US and European lenders exiting the crowded segment as local players step up.

title: Chip Stocks Put Up Their Best Quarter Ever With Some Wild Swings
summary: Chip stocks posted their best quarter ever, extending an extraordinary start to the year driven by insatiable demand for artificial intelligence equipment. But after recent jitters sent the stocks tumbling, investors are wondering how much further the rally can go.

title: Nike Posts Earnings That Surpass Expectations in Latest Quarter
summary: Nike Inc. reported better-than-expected results last quarter, a sign that Chief Executive Officer Elliott Hill’s turnaround efforts are gaining some traction.

title: S&P 500 Climbs at End of Best Quarter Since 2020: Markets Wrap
summary: The best quarter in six years for stocks ended on a positive note, with chipmakers extending their surge from war-driven lows and signs of economic resilience fueling optimism about corporate earnings.

title: US Stocks Gain as S&P 500, Nasdaq Notch Best Quarter Since 2020
summary: US stocks advanced on Tuesday as the main indexes notched their best quarter in six years and traders parsed the latest economic data.

title: Rising Inflation Is Squeezing the South
summary: The "Southern squeeze" is affecting the South, where the cost of living is rising and overall wages are failing to keep pace, with median household income in the South being about $76,000, well below the national average. This is forcing residents and businesses to make tough decisions. Brett Pulley reports for us from Atlanta.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Sanctions Mexican Companies for Cartel-Linked Fuel Smuggling
summary: The US Treasury announced sanctions against two Mexican citizens and nine companies in the country that the agency said are linked to a fuel-smuggling scheme with ties to a cartel.

title: Tungsten Can Deter China’s Military Dominance
summary: Around 80% of the world’s tungsten, used in bullets and armor plating, is produced by China. Bloomberg Opinion columnist David Fickling went to a remote mine in Australia to learn why that’s about to change. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasury Market’s June Rally Bails Out Quarter and First Half
summary: US Treasuries were set to finish June with a modest gain thanks to a collapse in inflation expectations, ending the market’s performance drought over the first five months of the year.

title: The Lower Income Consumer Has Felt Inflation The Most Says Suzuki
summary:
The best quarter in six years for stocks is ending on a positive note, with chipmakers extending their surge from war-driven lows and signs of economic resilience fueling optimism about corporate earnings.
A rally that’s added over $8 trillion to the S&P 500’s value in three months powered ahead as data signaled strength in both jobs and consumer sentiment. Dan Suzuki, Global Investment Strategist at iCapital joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Falls as Traders Weigh Middle East Return, Supply Glut Risks
summary: Oil declined with shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picking up amid hopes for a permanent US-Iran peace deal and new warnings of a near-term glut.

title: Domicile Isn't a Real Argument For Citizenship Says Wydra
summary: A divided US Supreme Court upheld the constitutional right of birthright citizenship, rejecting President Donald Trump’s planned restrictions and invalidating a central plank of his immigration agenda. Justices Clarence Thomas, Samuel Alito and Neil Gorsuch dissented. "National citizenship was not an independent concept but simply derivative of state citizenship. “Every citizen of a state,” it was often said, was “ipso facto a citizen of the United States.” In these contexts, too, national citizenship required domicile because state citizenship required domicile." Justice Thomas wrote. Elizabeth Wydra, President of the Constitutional Accountability Center joined Balance of Power to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Copper Climbs as Traders Eye Hormuz and Fed Rate Outlook
summary: Copper climbed as as traders awaited fresh peace talks between the US and Iran and clues on the outlook for US monetary policy.

title: China Opportunity: Where Global Capital Moves Next
summary: Brian Roberts, Head of Equities Product Development, Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing Ltd. told Bloomberg's Charlotte Yang how Hong Kong powers northbound flows. While on our panel Mark Konyn, Group Chief Investment Officer, AIA; Jeff Li, Chief Investment Officer, E Fund HK and Helen Qiao, Chief Greater China Economist & Head of Asia Economics Research, Bank of America shared their optimism for global investment in China with Bloomberg's Rebecca Sin at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Colombia Raises Interest Rate to 12% After Court Ends Crisis
summary: Colombia restarted interest rate rises after a court stripped the government of its power to block monetary tightening and the presidential election eased fears over central bank independence.

title: Oil Falls as Traders Weigh Middle East Return, Supply Glut Risks
summary: Oil declined, with shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz picking up on hopes for a permanent US-Iran deal and a Wall Street bank issuing fresh warnings about a potential glut. Michelle Brouhard  Head of Policy and Geopolitical Risk at Kpler joins to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB’s Dolenc Sees No Urgency to Hike If Energy Markets Stay Calm
summary: The European Central Bank may be able to keep interest rates unchanged at its next meeting if the situation in the Middle East doesn’t escalate in the meantime, according to Governing Council member Primoz Dolenc.

title: Susquehanna Says It Lost Millions to Mystery Insider Traders
summary: Susquehanna Investment Group is attempting to unmask the identities of individuals it claims made at least $100 million trading on inside information about a Chinese government crackdown on cross-border brokerages last month.

title: Chips Lead a Stock Rally | Open Interest 6/30/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Stocks are on pace for their best quarter in six years as chipmakers power the rally. The yen sinks to a four-decade low, oil heads for a quarterly drop, and we break down the latest Supreme Court rulings reshaping politics and immigration. Plus, Silas Brown on why sovereign wealth funds are pouring into private credit, and Nishant Kumar on Millennium’s billion-dollar bet on a new quant hedge fund. NOTE: National Public Radio issued a correction and retracted the story on Justice Alito. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bitcoin Slides Below $60,000 as Strategy Selloff Refuels Anxiety
summary: Bitcoin tumbled more than 3% toward $58,000 on Tuesday as investors reversed an initial vote of confidence in Michael Saylor’s financing overhaul at Strategy Inc., raising fresh concerns that one of the cryptocurrency’s biggest buyers may no longer be a consistent source of demand.

title: Sable Sinks 55% as Oil Driller Struggles to Repay Exxon Loan
summary: Sable Offshore Corp., briefly buoyed by a Trump administration push to boost oil production off the California coast, lost roughly half its market value after it turned to equity and bond investors to repay debt owed to Exxon Mobil Corp.

title: ECB’s Rehn Says Energy Shock Has Been ‘Clearly Stagflationary’
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Olli Rehn said the conflict in the Middle East is stoking inflation while weighing on economic expansion. Rehn spoke with Francine Lacqua on Tuesday in Sintra, Portugal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nike to Report With China, Tariff Refunds in Focus
summary: Poonam Goyal of Bloomberg Intelligence joins Bloomberg's Paul Sweeney and Alexis Christoforous to preview Nike earnings. Nike investors will look for further signs of progress in the company’s comeback effort as Chief Executive Officer Elliott Hill cements a leadership reset. Traders will also seek details about one-time tariff refunds and demand tied to the FIFA World Cup.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Erroneous Trades Force DTCC to Take a Rare Double-Settlement Day
summary: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corp., which settles most trades of US securities, said it will complete two days worth of transactions on Tuesday after a significant number of erroneous trades were submitted by a member firm.

title: Arabica Sees Largest Gain Since 2022 as Brazil Supply Risks Rise
summary: Arabica coffee jumped the most in almost four years, buoyed by mounting concerns about supply in top-grower Brazil.

title: UK ‘Minded to Intervene’ in Paramount’s Warner Bros. Deal Over Media Ownership Concerns
summary: Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. could face an intervention from the UK government after the country’s culture and media secretary raised concerns over the diversity of media ownership.

title: Gold Edges Above $4,000 as Traders Eye Fed Outlook, Iran Talks
summary: Gold edged above $4,000 an ounce, as traders weighed the outlook for US monetary policy following a fresh slew of economic data.

title: European Stocks Cap Best Quarter Since 2020 With Record High
summary: European stocks advanced on Tuesday, closing at a record high as a revival of the AI trade added momentum to a rally that has delivered the best quarter since late 2020.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/30/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: RBI Funding Curbs Deal ‘Body Blow’ to Indian Prop Trading Firms
summary: India’s proprietary trading firms and stock brokers are bracing for a major overhaul of how they finance trading activity.

title: BDC Veteran Tannenbaum Plans Comeback to Industry Near ‘Crisis’
summary: Len Tannenbaum, who sold his $5 billion credit firm to distressed debt pioneer Oaktree almost a decade ago, is looking to raise a new fund to capitalize on turmoil in the $1.8 trillion private credit market.
